channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: ReadBench evaluates bypassing text tokenization by rendering long-context documents as images for vision-language models
item.description: The technique builds on Google's pixel-only CLIPPO model design
item.content:encoded:
The technique builds on Google's pixel-only CLIPPO model design

Original source: https://github.com/teamchong/pxpipe

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/f3hapn0c

ReadBench turns established text benchmarks into image pages to test how well vision-language models handle dense documents without any text tokens at all. The evaluation draws directly from the pixel-only approach first shown in Google's CLIPPO work and measures real degradation across short and long contexts when models must read rendered A4 pages instead of receiving clean tokens. 33 581 31 426 50.7K FB | @XEOPHON T( | @TEORTAXESTEX SZ | @SUCHENZANG LB #72 | @GIFFMANA

  • Views: 52,547
  • Likes: 597
  • Bookmarks: 437
  • Quotes: 6
  • Replies: 27
  • Posts: 6

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • Susan Zhang
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Florian Brand
hilarious if labs run DeepSeek-OCR style context compression on the back end, of course derived from much older works

item.title: Huawei paper reveals Kirin 2026 processor benchmarks, claiming 41% lower power and 37.5% smaller die area
item.description: The architectural improvements stem from Huawei's new LogicFolding technique.
item.content:encoded:
The architectural improvements stem from Huawei's new LogicFolding technique.

Original source: https://x.com/poezhao0605/status/2073300079923069104

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/odrhuhww

Huawei's new paper from executive He Tingbo introduces LogicFolding as a circuit-level trick that stacks logic tiers to cut signal delays, delivering the reported gains on the Kirin 2026 versus the Kirin 9030 Pro at iso-performance while tying into a broader Tau scaling framework meant to complement Moore's Law. 18 188 12 62 64.3K ZE | @ZEPHYR_Z9 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 66,786
  • Likes: 196
  • Bookmarks: 63
  • Quotes: 4
  • Replies: 14
  • Posts: 6

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Zephyr
Tau Law V2. This confused me for a moment but it's at iso-performance. So with LogicFolding, Huawei can actually do a pretty cool thing, they raise the ceiling for free while opening a new tier of much colder high-end operation. The current 9030 Pro will be wholly obsolete.

item.title: David Sacks warns that enterprises feeding data to AI model providers risk fueling their future competitors
item.description: Chamath Palihapitiya backed the warning, urging firms to build independent AI systems.
item.content:encoded:
Chamath Palihapitiya backed the warning, urging firms to build independent AI systems.

Original source: https://x.com/TheChiefNerd/status/2073201584251486213

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/myxqr0tn

David Sacks flagged a concrete risk for companies uploading proprietary data, trade secrets, and customer information to frontier AI labs: those same providers could later weaponize the transferred knowledge to launch competing products in verticals like design, code, or security. Chamath Palihapitiya amplified the point, noting the pattern already visible in sectors where AI can accelerate returns on capital employed. 75 2.2K 153 732 196.2K CP | @CHAMATH DS #192 | @DAVIDSACKS

  • Views: 89,689
  • Likes: 456
  • Bookmarks: 68
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 28
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • David Sacks
  • Chamath Palihapitiya
I believe this 💯.

item.title: Mikhail Parakhin and Elon Musk argue abundant compute is actively eroding traditional AI technological moats
item.description: They suggest minor hardware scale differences now separate top-tier performers.
item.content:encoded:
They suggest minor hardware scale differences now separate top-tier performers.

Original source: https://x.com/tunguz/status/2073170944357798373

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/k76vhzbt

Shopify CTO Mikhail Parakhin and Elon Musk are weighing in on how easy access to large-scale compute is flattening the old edges that used to protect top AI labs, with the result that only small hardware differences now separate leaders from the pack. 100 453 41 22 40.1K MP | @MPARAKHIN EM #52 | @ELONMUSK

  • Views: 41,801
  • Likes: 463
  • Bookmarks: 23
  • Quotes: 5
  • Replies: 96
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Elon Musk
  • Mikhail Parakhin
Unfortunately, close to being true. We used to say: “The difference between the champs and the chimps is 20% compute”.

item.title: Meta's Lucas Beyer and Susan Zhang argue the boundary between self-supervised and unsupervised learning remains contested
item.description: Practitioners say industry terminology marginalizes traditional unsupervised methods.
item.content:encoded:
Practitioners say industry terminology marginalizes traditional unsupervised methods.

Original source: https://x.com/cloneofsimo/status/2073338859577114747

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ad8pg7ja

Prominent AI researchers are resurfacing old internal disagreements about where self-supervised learning ends and unsupervised learning begins, with no clean line emerging from the recollections. The conversation centers on how teams once argued over definitions without reaching black-and-white conclusions, especially as newer frameworks prompt fresh rounds of naming and positioning. 11 123 1 31 21.8K SR | @CLONEOFSIMO SZ | @SUCHENZANG LB #72 | @GIFFMANA

  • Views: 477
  • Likes: 12
  • Replies: 2
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • Susan Zhang
  • Simo Ryu
@gabriberton JEPA is literally the only new idea in the SSL space in the past 3 years... what other SSL approaches are there? DINO? iBOT? MAE? these are all older than 3 years!

item.title: Meta researcher Lucas Beyer says his Fable5 chats haven't been downgraded, sparking debate over silent backend model routing
item.description: Engineer Florian Brand questioned if chats are quietly routed to alternative models.
item.content:encoded:
Engineer Florian Brand questioned if chats are quietly routed to alternative models.

Original source: https://x.com/giffmana/status/2073342166135369831/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/eu8expiv

Meta researcher Lucas Beyer noted steady results across his Fable 5 sessions and shared the observation with a distressed Spongebob meme, yet the comment prompted immediate pushback on whether Anthropic's backend might quietly substitute other models without alerting users. 12 94 1 2 4.1K FB | @XEOPHON LB #72 | @GIFFMANA

  • Views: 4,230
  • Likes: 97
  • Bookmarks: 2
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 11
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Lucas Beyer (bl16)
  • Florian Brand
@giffmana You sure your chats don’t just get routed?
